page: Amazon Web Services r7g.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features a single dedicated physical core on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 CPU model running at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for single-threaded workloads but notable limitations in multi-threaded and high-throughput scenarios. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores are rated as average, supported by average single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core fall into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor results, with uncached large-block read speeds at 30.66 GB/sec and write speeds at 10.88 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7g.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the ARM64 Graviton ecosystem with a generous memory allocation relative to its compute capacity. However, the single-core limitation presents a clear tradeoff, restricting its utility for parallel processing or high-concurrency workloads. This instance is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, small-scale caching layers, Redis development environments, or simple background tasks that require a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances without demanding intensive CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.medium is a memory-optimized instance powered by a single-core ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 8.0 GB of memory, providing a high 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU execution, as seen in Geekbench and stress-ng tests, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited due to the single-vCPU constraint. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight databases, small caching nodes, Redis development, and memory-bound background tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, ideal for lightweight caching and database tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.medium is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads and caching applications.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 core running at 2.6 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with zero local storage
Average single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core capacity
Best suited for lightweight caching, Redis development, and memory-bound tasks
